sections.paragraph:
suff. כַּםְפִּי m.-

(1) silver, so called from its pale colour (like the Gr. ἄργυρος, from ἀργὸς, white, and on the other hand זָהָב gold, from its tawny colour, comp. צָהַב). Gen 23:15, אַרְבַּע מֵאֹת שֶׁקֶל־כֶּסֶף “four hundred shekels of silver;” more often, however without the word שֶׁקֶל, e.g. אֶלֶף כֶּסֶף “a thousand (shekels) of silver,” Gen 20:16 עֶשְׂרִים כֶּסֶף “twenty (shekels) of silver,” Gen 37:28 Deu 22:19, 29 Deu 22:29 Hos 3:2.

(2) money, from silver, weighed out in small unstamped pieces, having been anciently used for money (comp. ἀργύριον, argent). Gen 23:13 Deu 23:20. Also used of that which is acquired by money, Exo 21:21 of a slave כִּי כַסֳפֹּו הוּא “for he is his money.” Pl. pieces of silver, money, Gen 42:25, 35 Gen 42:35.
